In this video, I wanted to show you around the Creator Studio really quick, specifically how we're going to go in and look at some of the data from this, because I think it's a little bit confusing how it's set up. So we're in the Creator Studio, and first thing you notice is you can select your different pages here. So we have Ezra's page, Molly's page, and our smart marketer page. So there's actually three that we really kind of want to look at whenever we dive into this data. You can also toggle over to look at Creator Studio for Instagram, not something I really use, but good to set up if you're doing this day to day. But you might think that Insights is where all the data is going to live. And there's definitely some information in here, right? Especially a lot of stuff on video is what you find, because the pages and audience overview are not particularly useful. This can be great if you want to dive in and see kind of what the engagement is on different videos, get a feel for all that. But what I really recommend you all look at is this content library option, because this shows you the post, and this is where you can see the impressions, engagement, all that stuff. So you can see here we've got things from Ezra's page and Molly's page. And this is the place where we can come and manually calculate that engagement to impression ratio. This post is just one of today. So it's not surprising that this isn't that much traction. This one went up yesterday. Not as good as far as the engagement ratio. We can actually calculate this. 22, about 2, 3, 9, 6. So just at the 1% mark, just under it really. Not too bad. That's kind of what we want to shoot for. This one on the other hand is looking way stronger. This is one of Ezra's notes to self. I've been doing this series for a while. And so we can calculate this one pretty easily. 2.9% engagement rate. Really, really good. Super strong. So that's a pretty great piece of content there. And then this one, yeah, it's right above 1%. Only so, so reach. We probably want to promote that one a little bit more maybe. But it's really nice to have these as a place to kind of dive in and see how different posts you're doing. And you can obviously look at any date range. That's the last week. And we talked about this already, but we like to do the weekly, the monthly, and the quarterly look back. You can dive in and see old posts, figure out what post type are working. So this one didn't get a lot of engagement. And I think that's something I find is a lot of our video clips don't get as much engagement. People really seem to react more to static images, even though videos have their own power and are definitely worth considering. But yeah, really, really nice tool to use. And just I wanted to go over it because it's a little hard to find sometimes. So when you're in the Creator Studio for your Facebook page or Instagram, you just want to go to the content library posts and you can find all this information. So hopefully that's helpful and great to calculate that engagement ratio and get a feel for how your different posts are doing on the Facebook platform.
